_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — TIER BREAK from Tier 1 (Levy) to Tier 2 (Conscript Sergeant). Take the L3 'Veteran Levy' composited keeper and produce the L4 'Conscript Sergeant' variant.
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Conscript Sergeant beat = 5 visible adds (iron banding rings on wooden mace head, scale-mail short sleeves on both upper arms, continuous iron rim band on shield, second steel pauldron on shield-arm side matching L3 mace-arm pauldron, steel chest-strap rivet plate). Tier-break composite will use --dilate 15. Within-tier chained input strategy (Rule 2): two inputs = L3 composite + L1_Base scale anchor. Includes CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION lead block listing all L2 + L3 features that must survive (sleeves, leather belt, hip pouch, rim rivets, single mace-arm pauldron, mace grip cord, four shield cross-bands, knife sheath).

v2 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0 — REMOVED steel chest-strap rivet plate (surface ornament with low thumbnail readability per Detail Economy rule; chest-strap-and-rivet read was already noisy alongside the existing pauldron rivets and iron banding). REPLACED with iron toe-cap strip on each boot per AD 'boot armor split into smaller upgrades, distributed earlier' (precedes ankle plates at L6 and full sabatons at L8). Updated the CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION block: removed knife-sheath line (L3 knife sheath was dropped per AD), added L3 steel skullcap line (first metal headpiece introduced at L3 per AD 'helmet much earlier'). Negatives updated to lock out knife/dagger sheath as a second belt accessory and to lock out full sabatons / ankle plates / greaves at L4.
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v4 (2026-05-07): updated downstream preserve/replace references for L2 rivets — 8-ring → 4 cardinal-point per AD detail-economy.

v5 (2026-05-07): the L4 iron rim band is now a fresh introduction (not a replacement of L2 rivets) since the L2 IRON RIM RIVETS positive add was dropped entirely. Shield was rivetless at L2/L3; L4 introduces continuous iron rim band cleanly.

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.
_meta.tier: Tier 2 — Conscript Sergeant (TIER BREAK from Tier 1)
_meta.input_image: UnitProgression/Infantry/out/v1/L3/composite/infantry_L3_v1_composited.png (L3 keeper denoised + composited against L2 keeper)

adds.title: IRON BANDING RINGS ON THE WOODEN MACE HEAD
adds.summary: add three iron rings circling the bulbous wooden mace head between the spike rows.

adds.title: SCALE-MAIL SHORT SLEEVES ON BOTH UPPER ARMS
adds.summary: add chunky overlapping rounded scale-shaped plates in cool silver-gray steel covering both upper arms from the shoulder down to mid-bicep, sitting OVER the existing L2 quilted leather short sleeves.

adds.title: CONTINUOUS IRON RIM BAND AROUND THE SHIELD PERIMETER
adds.summary: add a continuous dark cool-gray iron rim band wrapping the entire perimeter of the round shield, approximately half an inch wide, sitting OUTSIDE the existing ring of L2 rim rivets (the rivets are now contained within or just inside the new rim band).

adds.title: SECOND STEEL PAULDRON ON THE SHIELD-ARM SHOULDER
adds.summary: add a steel pauldron cap on the anatomical-LEFT shoulder (shield-arm side, viewer-RIGHT) matching the existing L3 pauldron on the anatomical-RIGHT shoulder.

adds.title: IRON TOE-CAP STRIP ON EACH BOOT
adds.summary: add a single dark cool-gray iron strip wrapping the FRONT of each boot toe, approximately 1 inch tall and 2 inches wide, riveted at the seam where it meets the warm-brown leather.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Infantry/out/v1/L3/composite/infantry_L3_v1_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the 'Veteran Levy' kit accumulated on top of the existing base. You MUST preserve every + feature exactly as shown in the input image: (a) QUILTED BROWN LEATHER UNDER-TUNIC SHORT SLEEVES on both upper arms (matte warm-brown leather, diagonal cross-stitching) — these stay UNDER the new scale-mail sleeves; (b) WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle centered at front; (c) SMALL BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH with flap and brass stud on the anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT side) — this is the ONLY belt accessory; (d) STEEL SKULLCAP visible UNDER the mail coif as a small cool-gray steel dome at the very top of the head plus a thin steel rim band visible across the brow line just inside the coif's facial opening, two small iron rivets at the temples — this is the first metal headpiece, introduced at this level; (e) SINGLE STEEL PAULDRON CAP on the anatomical-RIGHT shoulder (mace-arm, viewer-LEFT) — the new second pauldron arrives on the OTHER shoulder, this existing one stays where it is; (f) LEATHER GRIP CORD wrapping the upper third of the mace haft; (g) FOUR IRON STRAP-BANDS radiating from the central steel boss in a cross pattern on the wooden shield face. DO NOT revert any of these features. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the input image, marking the TIER BREAK from Tier 1 Levy to Tier 2 Conscript Sergeant. The new kit introduces metal at the SLEEVES (not yet on the chest) and brings the second shoulder + the shield rim into a fully metalled silhouette while keeping the red+mustard split livery tunic visibly DOMINANT on the chest. (1) IRON BANDING RINGS ON THE WOODEN MACE HEAD — add three iron rings circling the bulbous wooden mace head between the spike rows. The wooden mace head itself stays warm-brown wood; the six existing iron spikes stay cool-gray steel; the new iron rings are dark cool-gray steel banding, each ring approximately half an inch wide, evenly spaced around the head between and under the spike rows. (2) SCALE-MAIL SHORT SLEEVES ON BOTH UPPER ARMS — add chunky overlapping rounded scale-shaped plates in cool silver-gray steel covering both upper arms from the shoulder down to mid-bicep, sitting OVER the existing quilted leather short sleeves. Each scale is a small rounded shield-shape (semi-circular at the top, slightly tapered at the bottom), arranged in horizontal rows where each row overlaps the row below by half a scale. The texture reads as chunky, hand-painted, clearly individual scales — NOT chainmail rings, NOT fine ring mesh, NOT fish-scale fine texture, NOT a quilted diamond pattern. The scale-mail sleeves match the existing mail coif's scale style exactly. The scale sleeves end at mid-bicep; the existing quilted leather under-tunic sleeve is still briefly visible as a narrow ~1-inch band of warm-brown leather between the lower edge of the scale-mail sleeve and the upper edge of each leather forearm bracer. (3) CONTINUOUS IRON RIM BAND AROUND THE SHIELD PERIMETER — add a continuous dark cool-gray iron rim band wrapping the entire perimeter of the round shield, approximately half an inch wide, sitting just inside the outer edge of the wooden shield perimeter and wrapping the entire rim. The four iron cross strap-bands radiating from the boss remain visible against the wooden plank face between the central steel boss and the new continuous iron rim. The wooden plank face of the shield is still visible between the cross arms and the rim. (4) SECOND STEEL PAULDRON ON THE SHIELD-ARM SHOULDER — add a steel pauldron cap on the anatomical-LEFT shoulder (shield-arm side, viewer-RIGHT) matching the existing pauldron on the anatomical-RIGHT shoulder. Both pauldrons are now slightly LARGER than the existing single cap (approximately 5 inches in diameter, up from 4 inches at this level) — the silhouette mightiness rule applies: the shoulder envelope should read visibly broader than at this level. The pauldrons are cool silver-gray polished steel, slight domed curve, with three or four visible dark-iron rivets along each pauldron's lower seam. Each pauldron rivets to a thin warm-brown leather strap mostly hidden under the livery tunic. (5) IRON TOE-CAP STRIP ON EACH BOOT (FIRST BOOT ARMOR) — add a single dark cool-gray iron strip wrapping the FRONT of each boot toe, approximately 1 inch tall and 2 inches wide, riveted at the seam where it meets the warm-brown leather. Both boots get a matching iron toe-cap. The brown leather body of each boot is otherwise unchanged — leather visible at the calf, heel, and mid-foot. This is the FIRST boot armor on the unit; ankle plates arrive at and full continuous sabatons arrive at this level (three smaller adds across the chain instead of one big sabaton step). ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline; his stern flat closed-mouth expression; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with single bright catchlight; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin with rosy blush; his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering chin and lower cheeks reaching upper neck — trimmed short, NOT bushy, NOT wild, NOT long; his dark mustache continuous with the beard; his dark-brown short hair as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front of the mail coif; the chunky scale-mail coif covering scalp, ears, and neck down to collarbone; the sleeveless mustard-yellow and crimson-red split LIVERY TUNIC — viewer-LEFT half solid crimson-red, viewer-RIGHT half mustard-yellow with a vertical crimson-red center stripe — this red+mustard split chest livery remains visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible, NOT covered by scale mail (scale mail is at the SLEEVES only at this level, NOT on the chest), NOT shrunk into a thin strip; the existing brown leather forearm bracers wrapping wrist to mid-forearm on both arms; the existing quilted leather short sleeves under the new scale-mail sleeves (briefly visible at the bicep gap); the existing wide warm-brown leather belt with square dark-iron buckle; the existing brown leather hip pouch on the anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT) — the ONLY belt accessory, NO knife sheath / dagger / second pouch; the existing steel skullcap visible under the mail coif (small cool-gray steel dome at the very top of the head + thin steel rim band at the brow line inside the coif's facial opening + two small iron rivets at the temples); the existing leather grip cord wrapping the upper third of the mace haft; the spiked wooden mace held in the anatomical-RIGHT hand — heavy warm-brown wooden haft, bulbous wooden mace head, six visible iron spikes in steel, mace head dangling at hip height angled down toward viewer-LEFT (the new three iron banding rings per Edit Goal item 1 are added between and around the spike rows; the wooden mace head shape and the six steel spikes remain unchanged); the round wooden buckler-style shield held in the anatomical-LEFT arm — approximately 24 inches in diameter, warm-brown wooden plank face with faint vertical wood grain, central cool-gray steel boss as a small dome, the existing four iron cross strap-bands radiating from the boss; the dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers slightly loose at the cuff; the chunky warm-brown leather boots reaching mid-calf with darker folded ankle cuff. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE MACE ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, elbow bent ~120 degrees, hand grips the upper haft just below the mace head, mace head dangling at hip height angled down toward viewer-LEFT. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, forearm angled across the body bringing the round shield up to upper-chest / shoulder height with the shield's flat face presenting toward the viewer at slight three-quarter angle. Calm guard stance — both arms static and balanced, NOT a swing, NOT a charge. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire character in deep warm brown-black, slight thickening in shadowed recesses, slight thinning where forms catch the key light. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 66.8% of canvas vertical height (~684px tall) and approximately 60.8% of canvas horizontal width (~623px wide, including the mace and shield). TOP of mail coif at approximately 16.6% from top of canvas. BOTTOM of boots at approximately 16.6% from bottom of canvas. Character vertically centered. Approximately 18.3% empty margin viewer-LEFT, 20.9% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT revert any or feature: DO NOT remove the quilted leather sleeves; DO NOT replace the leather belt with a rope cord; DO NOT remove the hip pouch, mace haft grip cord, or shield cross-bands; DO NOT remove the existing steel skullcap visible under the mail coif (steel dome at top + thin steel rim band at brow line + two iron rivets at temples must all remain visible); DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, scabbard, or any second belt accessory at this level — the existing hip pouch is the ONLY belt accessory through the entire chain; DO NOT remove the existing single steel pauldron on the mace-arm shoulder (the existing second pauldron arrives on the OTHER shoulder); DO NOT remove or shrink or cover the red+mustard split livery tunic — it MUST remain visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible BETWEEN the two pauldrons and AROUND the new chest-strap rivet plate; DO NOT cover the tunic chest with scale mail, chain mail, breastplate, or any body armor — scale mail at is at the SLEEVES ONLY, NOT on the chest; DO NOT extend scale mail to the upper chest, lower chest, or replace the tunic; DO NOT extend scale mail downward into a long skirt past the tunic hem — that arrives at this level; DO NOT replace the wooden mace head with a flanged steel head — the wood + spikes + new iron banding rings configuration is the existing mace; DO NOT change the iron spike count or arrangement on the mace head; DO NOT add iron banding to the haft (haft reinforcement strip arrives at this level); DO NOT change the round shield to a heater shield, kite shield, tower shield, square shield, or any non-round shape; DO NOT replace the wooden shield face with metal — that arrives at this level (steel-faced); DO NOT add an embossed sun-burst boss or sun-burst boss pattern — those arrive at this level; DO NOT add a steel breastplate, cuirass, plackart, or any chest plate — those arrive at this level+; DO NOT add a bevor / steel collar / steel throat plate — those arrive at this level; DO NOT add a helm, sallet, kettle helm, or any structural addition to the head — head covering remains the existing mail coif; DO NOT add steel vambraces or replace the leather forearm bracers — those arrive at this level; DO NOT add ankle plates, full continuous sabatons, greaves, knee plates, tassets, fauld, or any leg / hip armor at this level — the existing boot addition is ONLY a single iron toe-cap strip on each boot (ankle plates arrive at this level, full continuous toe+foot+ankle sabatons arrive at this level, tassets at this level); DO NOT extend the iron toe-cap up the foot or around the ankle at this level; DO NOT add a steel chest-strap rivet plate, central chest disc, or any chest-mounted plate at this level (removed 2026-05-07 — was surface ornament with low thumbnail readability); DO NOT make the pauldrons layered multi-disc — at each pauldron is a single rounded steel cap (slightly larger than the existing pauldron disc visible in the input image); layered 2-disc pauldrons arrive at this level; DO NOT add a second mace-arm pauldron piece — exactly ONE steel cap per shoulder at this level; DO NOT make the chest-strap rivet plate larger than ~3 inches; DO NOT extend the chest-strap rivet plate into a full breastplate; DO NOT make the existing scale-mail sleeves into chainmail rings or fine ring mesh — they MUST be chunky overlapping rounded scale plates matching the mail coif scale style; DO NOT make the iron banding rings on the mace head into smooth tubes — they are flat iron banding strips wrapping the wood; DO NOT make the iron rim band on the shield wider than half an inch; DO NOT add gold trim, gold rivets, gold band, gold buckle, gold accents, or any gold anywhere — first gold appears at this level; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT add horns, crests, plumes, or vertical helm extensions (no helm at this level); DO NOT change any facial feature; DO NOT grow the beard bushy or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT change the hair, the hood/coif color, the livery colors, the trouser color, or the boot color; DO NOT change the chibi proportions or head-to-body ratio; DO NOT swap the hand holding the mace; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white — the background must be clean opaque white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
